normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU1_nonempty_precondition (arr : Arr) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∃ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = sliceSum arr i j := by
  refine ⟨0, 1, ?_, rfl⟩
  exact Nat.zero_lt_one
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice is non-empty; establish that there exists at least one non-empty contiguous range to witness (i < j).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU2_witness_exists (arr : Arr) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∃ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a contiguous non-empty subslice (indices i..j with i < j) whose sum equals the returned maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU3_upper_bound (arr : Arr) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∀ i j : Nat, i < j → sliceSum arr i j ≤ maxSubarray arr
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: For every contiguous non-empty subslice, its sum is not strictly greater than the returned value.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem SU4_contiguity_form (arr : Arr) (h : arr ≠ []) : ∃ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The witness is expressed as a contiguous range i..j (not an arbitrary subsequence), with i < j.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem SU5_all_negative_case (arr : Arr) (hne : arr ≠ []) (hallneg : ∀ x ∈ arr, x < 0) :
  maxSubarray arr = (arr.foldr (fun x acc => max x acc) (Int.minBound))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: If every element is negative, the maximum subarray sum equals the maximum single element.
